title: anthemis orientalis f. ligulata
intro: Anthemis orientalis f. ligulata, also known as Golden Chamomile, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its golden yellow daisy-like flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing effects. The health benefits of Anthemis orientalis f. ligulata include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and alleviating insomnia, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Golden Chamomile include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used internally or applied topically for therapeutic purposes.
